title: lamium galeobdolon subsp. galeobdolon
intro: Lamium galeobdolon subsp. galeobdolon, also known as yellow archangel or wood betony, is a type of flowering plant in the mint family Lamiaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic effects, as well as its ability to calm the nervous system and reduce anxiety. The health benefits of yellow archangel include its potential to treat digestive issues, relieve headaches and migraines, and soothe respiratory problems such as bronchitis and asthma. In herbal preparations, yellow archangel can be consumed as a tea, taken as a tincture, or used as a topical application to promote healing and relaxation.
